issue_description: England-born immigrant endpoint with parent PIDs present downstream.
proposed_handling: Include Michael as endpoint; exclude parents from strict immigrant-limited analysis unless line deliberately reopened.
notes: Model case for Lucy immigrant-boundary overrun.

issue_description: FamilySearch-derived record has birth date but no birthplace; user found origin data in Ancestry.com.
proposed_handling: Create accepted birthplace overlay after exact wording/source is recorded; patch/test builder later so override_birth_place applies to slotmap output.
analysis_effect: Can become strict usable after accepted overlay and builder support.
evidence_or_basis: User Ancestry.com review, exact wording pending.
notes: Do not patch builder tonight; planned next-session implementation.

issue_description: No own birth data in slotmap; parents Henry Haggett and Ann Hannah Pinder are English immigrants.
proposed_handling: Do not create strict birthplace without source; optional expanded-analysis annotation as likely domestic/colonial.
evidence_or_basis: Parent context: Henry Haggett and Ann Hannah Pinder listed as English-born.
notes: Parent-based inference stronger than spouse-only context but still not a raw fact.

issue_description: No birth year/place; married to Hannah Martin, a non-immigrant colonial-line spouse.
proposed_handling: Preserve broken-branch status; optional low/medium-confidence domestic/colonial annotation only.
analysis_effect: Expanded/sensitivity analysis only; not strict without evidence.
notes: Also a broken branch with no parents.

issue_description: First name only but own England birth is present.
proposed_handling: Include as-is using own birthplace; preserve missing surname limitation.
notes: Name quality issue, not origin blocker.

issue_description: First name only but own England birth is present.
proposed_handling: Include as-is using own birthplace; preserve missing surname limitation.
notes: Name quality issue, not origin blocker.

issue_description: Rows with PID but no name/demographics, likely many from Navigator queue artifact or unharvested parents.
proposed_handling: Preserve structurally but exclude from strict Phase 2 analyses unless reviewed and harvested.
analysis_effect: Do not count as demographic/origin data.
evidence_or_basis: Slotmap contains many PID-only rows in generation 6 and elsewhere.

issue_description: Repeated PIDs/couples indicate cousin marriage/pedigree collapse, not an error.
proposed_handling: Preserve duplicate slot occurrences; choose unique-person or slot-weighted mode by analysis.
analysis_effect: Unique-person mode for migration/photos; slot-weighted mode for composition/genetic-style questions.
evidence_or_basis: Matching PIDs appear in multiple slots.
